behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at a module boundary: summary-write path must read persisted per-dimension findings for the same round and enforce a cross-record constraint (MOCK-MISS). Unit was rejected because behavior text requires interaction with stored findings, not one pure function. System/Agentic/Workflow were rejected because the behavior text does not require OS/network/subprocess, real model output, or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary service + findings repository against a local test DB. Seed one round with at least one FAIL finding, attempt to persist PASS summary, assert write is rejected and persisted state remains non-PASS.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when gate logic and storage layer interact: summary text says PASS but stored findings include FAIL, and gate must trust stored findings. Unit was rejected because this is a data-source wiring failure across modules (MOCK-MISS). Higher levels were rejected since behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess or LLM-dependent correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Using real gate module + repository (local DB), store conflicting data (manual summary PASS, stored finding FAIL). Run stop-gate computation and assert verdict is FAIL/escalate based on stored findings, not summary text.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans policy enforcement plus audit persistence, so minimum boundary is module wiring (request/flag handling + audit store). Unit was rejected because a pure function test would miss missing-audit or persistence handoff failures. System/Agentic/Workflow were rejected: no required real infra, model judgment, or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise summary-update API/service with and without override flag using local persistence. Assert: without flag override is denied; with flag override is accepted and a warning/audit record is persisted with actor, round, and reason metadata.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is stale-state handoff between fix-commit metadata and review-round metadata; requires joining/version-order checks across modules/storage. Unit rejected because behavior text requires comparing entities from different subsystems. System rejected because text does not require real git subprocess/OS behavior, only commit-vs-round ordering semantics. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model-output dependence or required multi-agent sequence stated.
behaviors.test_description: Seed commits and review rounds in local DB: create fix commit C2 after prior reviewed commit C1, with latest round tied to C1 only. Attempt PASS finalize and assert block; create new round tied to C2 and assert PASS can proceed.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets orchestration guardrails (fresh-round existence on updated diff), not model output quality. Integration catches failures in loop state handoff between diff update, round scheduler, and gate. Agentic was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require evaluating what the model decides, only that a new round ran. Workflow was considered but rejected because this item requires a fresh round before finalize, not an exhausted multi-step chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the review-loop orchestrator with local fakes for agent executor and storage: update diff, skip triggering fresh round, assert PASS finalization is blocked; then trigger one fresh round for that diff and assert gate unblocks subject to findings.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower than Agentic is consistent because assertions are about run freshness/orchestration, not LLM judgment correctness.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is gate decision over stored findings + round-budget state; this is cross-module state evaluation. Unit rejected because unresolved findings and exhaustion typically come from separate persisted state sources. System/Agentic/Workflow rejected: no explicit need for real infra, model-quality assertions, or multi-agent sequence in this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: With local persistence, set remaining rounds to zero and seed unresolved MUST-FIX findings. Execute gate decision and assert escalation outcome and explicit non-PASS terminal status.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a live multi-round workflow with sequential round progression and terminal escalation logic; this is MULTI-STEP orchestration across rounds. Integration was rejected because behavior text says 'live multi-round review workflow,' which disqualifies a single-boundary wiring test. Agentic alone was rejected because this is not just one model decision but sequence-level control and terminal policy across rounds.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end review workflow over multiple rounds on updated diffs (real workflow runner), consume round budget, keep MUST-FIX unresolved in stored findings, and assert PASS remains blocked and escalation event/record is emitted at exhaustion.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow is required because the test must observe correctness across sequenced rounds, not one isolated gate call.
